message:
test(fr-runtime): fix memory_stats_returns_bulk to expect keyed-array reply (br-frankenredis-3kdz)

The test was the last failing case in `cargo test --workspace` and
had been treated as "pre-existing flaky" across multiple agent
sessions. Surfaced by /reality-check-for-project: it's actually a
test bug, not a runtime bug.

Upstream `object.c::memoryCommand` (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/object.c:1566)
replies to `MEMORY STATS` with a RESP map / RESP2-array of
(bulk-string-key, integer-value) pairs — `addReplyMapLen` followed by
a run of `addReplyBulkCString` + `addReplyLongLong` calls. The prior
test asserted a single bulk string body containing `key:value\n` lines
(mistaking MEMORY STATS for INFO-style output), which always failed
against our Redis-correct array reply. Our runtime was already shipping
the right shape.

* Rename: `memory_stats_returns_bulk` → `memory_stats_returns_keyed_array`.
* Decode the RESP array, assert non-empty + even-length pair structure.
* Pin the canonical upstream-documented keys (`peak.allocated`,
  `total.allocated`, `startup.allocated`) to catch silent label drift.
* Pin integer-valued keys to integer replies — guards against any
  future regression that accidentally emits paired bulk-string values
  where clients expect longs.

Validated via rch: cargo test -p fr-runtime --test admin_commands_test
42/42 (was 41 + 1 fail). cargo clippy -p fr-runtime --all-targets
-- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
